description: Uses flask[async] in requirements (required for async route handlers)
Auth0 domain written to .env config file
Client ID written to .env config file
Does not use wrong authentication libraries (Authlib, python-jose, Flask-Login, Flask-Dance)
Does not use JavaScript SPA SDK in Python server app
Does not use in-memory stores (production anti-pattern)

question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 into a Flask web application with: (1) ServerClient initialization with domain, client_id, client_secret, secret, and redirect_uri, (2) async route handlers using await, (3) session-based state and transaction stores, (4) login route calling start_interactive_login(), (5) callback route calling complete_interactive_login(), (6) profile route using get_user(), and (7) logout route calling logout()?
examples:
PASS: Uses ServerClient with all required params, FlaskSessionStateStore and FlaskSessionTransactionStore for session persistence, async def routes with await for all SDK calls, and has login/callback/profile/logout routes.
PASS: Uses async Flask routes with auth0-server-python ServerClient, custom store classes extending StateStore and TransactionStore, and complete auth flow.
FAIL: Uses Authlib or python-jose instead of auth0-server-python.
FAIL: Uses synchronous route handlers (def instead of async def) with SDK methods.
FAIL: Uses MemoryStateStore instead of session-based stores.
description: correctness-judge: Solution correctly integrates Auth0 session-based authentication in Flask

question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like ServerClient, start_interactive_login, complete_interactive_login, get_user, logout, import auth0_server_python). Look for at least 2 of: (1) FlaskSessionStateStore or FlaskSessionTransactionStore custom class implementations extending StateStore/TransactionStore, (2) flask[async] explicitly in requirements or install command, (3) SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE or SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY or SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE configuration, (4) encrypt/decrypt methods used in store implementations for JWE encryption. These are advanced patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard quickstart knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Code defines FlaskSessionStateStore extending StateStore with encrypt/decrypt AND has flask[async] in requirements.txt.
PASS: Code configures SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True AND implements FlaskSessionTransactionStore with JWE encryption.
FAIL: Code only uses basic ServerClient with start_interactive_login/logout — no custom stores, no flask[async], no session cookie config.
FAIL: Code uses MemoryStateStore from SDK instead of custom Flask session-based stores.
description: skill-attribution-judge: At least 2 skill-specific advanced patterns present (custom stores, flask[async], session security, JWE encryption)
Implements custom Flask session-based store classes (core skill differentiation)
Uses async route handlers with await for SDK calls
Configures Flask session cookie security settings
